abstract: The 2022 launch of ChatGPT triggered a wave of social media discussion about generative AI. Drawing on 215,279 posts from five months after this launch, we extend prior accounts of hype as a vehicle for strategic resource mobilization to develop a theory of hype as collective sensemaking and knowledge creation. Existing models rest on a fixed relational architecture: knowledgeable actors leverage information asymmetries to project visions, directing expectations top down. ChatGPT collapsed this architecture by making generative AI accessible, democratizing technology evaluation; meanwhile, social media emerged as the arena where relational publics could form, driving vision formation and contestation around generative AI from the bottom up, reshaping how a nascent technology acquired meaning. Combining grounded theory with computational text analysis, we develop a process model on the role of relational publics in hypes. We explain how collective knowledge accumulates through traceable conceptual prototyping, testing a technology’s capability and proposing meaning simultaneously, and how, when this knowledge crosses a technology literacy threshold, hype transitions from an expanding to a containing phase. The model advances our understanding of how novel technologies diffuse when crowds simultaneously co-construct visionary imaginaries and practical knowledge, and of the role social media plays in this process.

ob.ai_reason: GenAI社交媒体讨论与集体知识建构，AI核心议题
ob.tldr: 通过分析 ChatGPT 发布后五个月内 215,279 条社交媒体帖子，揭示了 hype 作为集体感知与知识建构的角色：AI 的可及性让技术评估自下而上形成关系公众，知识通过概念原型累积，并在跨越技术素养阈值后 hype 由扩张转为收敛。该过程阐释了社交媒体在新技术扩散中同步共创想象与实践知识的机制。

title: ChatGPT, a Hype That Teaches: Generative AI and the Collective Accumulation of Technology Literacy on Social Media
title_zh: ChatGPT，一个具有教育意义的热潮：生成式AI与社交媒体上的技术素养集体积累
